titleStmt.title: SII 31.148: original edition by C. A. Padmanabha Sastri and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 148. (A.R. No. B 2 of 1937.) ŚRĪRANGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK AND DISTRICT.

summary.p: Engraved on the another slab (III) built into the pavement on the east of the first prākāra in the temple of Śrī Ranganāthasvāmi.
This undated record can be assigned to 18th century on internal and palaeographical evidences.

Refers to the prayers by Mangammavāru, Muddu Chandrasēkhammavāru Kamalājammavāru, Jānakammavāru and Induvadanammavāru, the queens of Chokkanāthanāyaka for the disapparance of his Chāya i.e. probably refers to the act of prayers at the time of his death.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: (Published in South Indaia Inscription Volume XXIV. Ins. No. 563, pp. 536-37.)

śrīrāya cokkanādhanāyani vā
riki maṁggammavāru muddu caṁ
draśērēkhammavāru kamalāja
mmavāru jānakaṁmmavāru Iṁ
duvadanammavāru vīru ĀIdu
gurunnu cokkanāthanāya
nivāri chāyānuve¨rttigānu
ṁḍa dayaśēsugāka vāri perimeṭṭe
na cokkanāthanāyanivāri Ihi
ka Āmuṣmikamullōvārini
nnavāri AIdugurulū
korukonna Aṭuvaṁṭṭi Ati
śayaṁ Emi śēyumaṁ
cu śrīraṁgganāyakula
caraṇakaṁjamulaku E vi
nnapaṁ śesukunnārō Ā vinna
paṁ śrīraṁgganāyakulu
daya śesugāka .
Digital edition of SII 31.148 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
